name: Cincinnati Chili (Like Empress Chili Parlor)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large heavy kettle, cook the onions and garlic in a little oil over moderate heat, stirring until the onions are softened.
2. Add the beef and cook the mixture, stirring and breaking up lumps, until the beef is no longer pink.
3. Stir in the chili powder, paprika, cumin, coriander, allspice, oregano, cinnamon, cloves, and mace for 1 minute.
4. Add the bay leaf, water, tomato sauce, vinegar, and molasses; stir occasionally and add more water if needed to keep beef barely covered.
5. Simmer uncovered until thickened but still soupy enough to ladle, about 2 hours.
6. Discard the bay leaf and season with salt and pepper.
7. Serve as is or in the traditional Cincinnati 5-way: over spaghetti topped with beans, onion, Cheddar cheese, and oyster crackers (also great on hot dogs).
